article_title: Nvidia Releases Ising, an Open-Source AI Model Family Aimed at Making Quantum Computers Actually Work

content_preview.title: Nvidia Releases Ising, an Open-Source AI Model Family Aimed at Making Quantum Computers Actually Work
content_preview.summary: Nvidia's new Ising family targets quantum error correction and calibration, claiming 2.5x faster and 3x more accurate decoding than the current open-source standard, with more than 20 labs and hardware makers already on board.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Nvidia has released Ising, a family of open-source AI models designed to tackle two of the hardest problems standing between today's noisy quantum processors and genuinely useful quantum computers: error correction and calibration. The company [announced the launch](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-launches-ising-the-worlds-first-open-ai-models-to-accelerate-the-path-to-useful-quantum-computers) on April 14, with the models distributed through GitHub, Hugging Face, and buil...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong quantum/AI article on Nvidia Ising open-source quantum AI model family.
editor_notes.source_verification: Nvidia press release VERIFIED via WebFetch: Ising Calibration VLM + Ising Decoding CNN, 2.5x speed/3x accuracy improvement over existing OSS, Harvard/Fermilab/LBNL/Infleqtion/IQM adoption, Jensen Huang quote, GitHub/HF/NIM availability all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All verified. Specific 35B parameter count and '3D CNN' specifics come from Tom's Hardware secondary source.
